label: agent-mcp-budget performance and correctness audit

findings.description: Double-counting of historical tokens when a cap has both scope AND window. getSnapshotValue adds both scope-prefixed totals (queryScopeTotals: ALL historical tokens) AND window totals (queryWindowTokens: window-filtered historical tokens excluding current). This means the 24h-window tokens within the scope are counted TWICE.
findings.fix_sketch: In getSnapshotValue (line 514), when cap.window is present and scope !== 'task', the scope-prefixed values should not include queryScopeTotals. Either: (1) use snap['inputTokens']/snap['outputTokens'] (in-memory only) for the base when a window is present, or (2) subtract queryScopeTotals from the base when adding window totals. The simplest fix: if (cap.window && (cap.scope ?? dimScope ?? 'task') !== 'task') { base = snap['inputTokens'] + snap['outputTokens'] + snap['cacheTokens']; } before the window addition at line 550.
findings.repro: Config: { caps: [{ field: 'tokens', maximum: 100000, window: 'PT24H', scope: 'session' }] }. Real DB has 80k historical tokens (all within 24h). Current task: 10k tokens. Enforcement calculates 170k vs 100k limit -> false positive BLOCK. Actual 24h total is 90k -> should PASS.

findings.description: Flat config maxTokensPer24h loses its agent scope. FIELD_MAP maps maxTokensPer24h to { field: 'tokens', window: 'PT24H' } without a scope. This creates a cap with implicit scope='task' (via dimScope fallback). queryWindowTokens returns 0 for scope='task' (no branch matches), so the 24h window DB query is effectively dead. Only the in-memory accumulator counts, meaning the cap silently applies only to the current task — a regression from the previous behavior where maxTokensPer24h was an agent-scoped rolling window.
findings.fix_sketch: Two options: (1) Add scope property to FIELD_MAP type and set maxTokensPer24h scope to 'agent'. (2) Hardcode scope:'agent' in flatFieldsToDimension for this specific key. Option 2 is simpler: check key === 'maxTokensPer24h' in flatFieldsToDimension and set cap.scope = 'agent'. Note: no test covers this path; the existing test at line 685 uses structured config with explicit scope: 'agent'.

findings.description: Cache tokens excluded from time-window DB queries. In getSnapshotValue for field='tokens' with a scope+window combination, snap['cacheTokens'] always reads the in-memory (current-task-only) value. The window query (queryWindowTokens at line 394) sums only input_tokens + output_tokens, excluding cache tokens entirely. This means cache tokens from historical tasks within the window are never counted against the budget cap.
findings.fix_sketch: Add cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens to the SUM in queryWindowTokens (lines 415, 427, 438) for all three scope branches, similar to how queryScopeTotals already includes them.

findings.description: No test for combined scope+window caps with real DB responses for BOTH queries. The existing maxTokensPer24h test (line 685) uses a mock DB that returns undefined for queryScopeTotals (no 'created_at' in SQL), so it never exercises the double-counting path. A real DB would return historical tokens from both queries, causing the double-counting bug described above.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a test with a mock DB that returns non-undefined for ALL queries (both scope totals and window totals), verifying that a cap with scope+window doesn't overcount.

findings.description: Accumulator cleanup tested only for task:completed; no tests for task:failed or task:cancelled paths. The onTerminal handler (line 263) dispatches to all three, and the orchestrator emits all three (lines 656, 683, 705), but only task:completed has a test (line 370).
findings.fix_sketch: Add test cases: emit task:failed / task:cancelled, then verify a new task with the same taskId creates a fresh accumulator (same pattern as line 370-399).

findings.description: No test for the global scope ('global') in any cap. The schema supports scope='global' and queryWindowTokens has a branch for it (line 432), but there's no test coverage for cross-agent caps.
findings.fix_sketch: Add a test with scope='global' using the mock DB, confirming the DB query excludes the current task and sums across all tasks.

findings.description: Named + default export warning. The build emits: 'Entry module is using named and default exports together.' createPlugin is both a named export and the default export (line 623-624). Consumers using ESM may import it as `import createPlugin from '...'` or `import { createPlugin } from '...'`. The default in CJS becomes `agent-mcp-budget.default.createPlugin`, which is unexpected.
findings.fix_sketch: Remove the named export on line 624: keep only `export default createPlugin;` or only `export { createPlugin };` but not both.

backlog_entries: BL-xxx — agent-mcp-budget: combined scope+window caps double-count historical tokens (HIGH). Fix: scope-prefixed totals should not include queryScopeTotals when a window is also present.
BL-xxx — agent-mcp-budget: flat-format maxTokensPer24h loses scope (MEDIUM). FIELD_MAP should set scope:'agent' for backward compatibility.
BL-xxx — agent-mcp-budget: cache tokens excluded from historical window queries (MEDIUM). queryWindowTokens only sums input_tokens + output_tokens.
BL-xxx — agent-mcp-budget: missing test coverage for scope+window double-counting, global scope, and failed/cancelled cleanup (MEDIUM/LOW).
